Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the verge of breaking Sachin Tendulkar's 36-year-old India record
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (BCCI Photo)

Sooryavanshi's remarkable rise



A chance to break Sachin Tendulkar 's record



'Words cannot explain it': Sooryavanshi



Will the wait end in Belfast?



Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think young players should get early opportunities in international cricket? Yes, If they earn it. No, Experience should come first. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

India vs Ireland T20Is: Squads

India

Ireland

NEW DELHI: All eyes will be on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi when India take on Ireland in the first T20I of the two-match series in Belfast on Friday.The teenage batting sensation has become one of the biggest talking points in world cricket, with fans eagerly waiting to see if he will make his India debut. If he gets a game, Sooryavanshi will create history by becoming the youngest player to represent India in men's international cricket.Sooryavanshi earned his maiden senior call-up after an impressive IPL 2026 season. Playing for Rajasthan Royals, he scored 776 runs in 16 innings at a staggering strike rate of 237. He also smashed 72 sixes, setting a new record for the most sixes in a single IPL season.The youngster carried his form into the India A tri-series in Sri Lanka. In the one-day final against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla, he blasted 94 off just 29 balls, once again showing why he is regarded as one of the brightest young talents in the game.At 15 years and 71 days, Sooryavanshi became the youngest player ever to be selected in India's senior men's squad, breaking Sachin Tendulkar's 36-year-old record.Tendulkar made his international debut in 1989 against Pakistan at the age of 16 years and 205 days.If Sooryavanshi is included in the playing XI against Ireland, he will surpass the batting legend to become India's youngest men's international cricketer.Speaking after receiving his maiden India call-up, Sooryavanshi described wearing the India jersey as a dream come true."Words cannot explain it. The reason I picked up the bat from day one and went to the ground for practice: that dream is fulfilled now. The biggest step in that journey was completed today. I truly cannot put this feeling into words," he said in a video shared by the BCCI.The teenager admitted he was overwhelmed when he first saw the India jersey."I felt like a dream when I saw that T-shirt; I couldn't stop smiling. Sometimes things happen that you never imagined could happen. When it finally does, you don't know how to react. That was exactly how I felt," he added.Whether he makes his debut on Friday or later in the series, excitement around Sooryavanshi continues to grow. For now, the biggest question ahead of India's clash against Ireland remains the same: Will the 15-year-old get his first India cap?: Shreyas Iyer (captain), Tilak Varma (vice-captain), Ravi Bishnoi, Abhishek Sharma, Suryansh Shedge, Prasidh Krishna, Sanju Samson, Axar Patel, Harshit Rana, Ishan Kishan, Washington Sundar, Arshdeep Singh, Shivam Dube, Prince Yadav, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.: Lorcan Tucker (captain), Ross Adair, Ben Calitz, Gareth Delany, George Dockrell, Stephen Doheny, Matthew Humphreys, Gavin Hoey, Matthew Hollard, Liam McCarthy, Jai Moondra, Harry Tector, Tim Tector, Reuben Wilson.
Article Summary: India take on Ireland in the first T20I of the two-match series in Belfast on Friday. If he gets a game, Sooryavanshi will become the youngest player to represent India in men's international cricket. The 15-year-old earned his maiden senior call-up after an impressive IPL 2026 season.

Article Title: 'None of our MPs will split': Sharad Pawar's bold assertion in season of defections
Article Text:
Sharad Pawar has asserted that Nationalist Congress Party (SP) will remain united

The defection claim

A small party but a big risk



The NCP reunion that never happened

Could history repeat?

NEW DELHI: "None of our MPs will split. Not a single one of our MLAs is going anywhere … " this was Nationalist Congress Party (SP) chief Sharad Pawar ’s confident, if not bold, assertion that his party remains united and will not tread the same path as Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena (UBT) or Mamata Banerjee’s Trinamool Congress.This comes in response to a claim by NCP MLA Dharmarao Atram that five of the eight MPs of Sharad Pawar's party are ready to cross over to the Nationalist Congress Party led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar by December.Pawar’s daughter Supriya Sule , who is a Lok Sabha member from Baramati and one of the eight party MPs, rejected the NCP MLA’s claim and dared him to disclose the names. "Atram might be knowing more. I do not have any idea. We eight MPs were united yesterday, are united today and will remain united tomorrow. If Baba Atram has a list of five MPs, he should disclose the names," Sule said.Sule also took a dig at Atram and quipped: "If he has a list of five MPs, why is he not poaching me? Why is he sparing me?"Sharad Pawar’s NCP-SP has at present 8 MPs and 10 MLAs - making the party vulnerable to poaching. Especially, at a time when at least two regional heavyweights, despite their tall claims of unity, have been dealt a crushing blow by the rebels.The Anti-defection Act, which provides parties a protective shield against defections, failed in both the cases as the rebels played the numbers game with perfection. The Anti-defection Act does not come into play when two-thirds of the legislators decide to change sides together. And that is what happened with both Shiv Sena (UBT) and the Trinamool Congress.In the case of Sharad Pawar’s party - a legally viable defection will require at least 6 MPs to quit together while in the case of MLAs the number would be 7.Sharad Pawar, who played a key role in the formation of the Maha Vikas Aghadi in 2019 to keep the BJP out of power in Maharashtra, has already lost control of his party and symbol to a revolt led by his nephew late Ajit Pawar in 2023. 40 out of the 55 NCP MLAs then sided with Ajit as a helpless Sharad Pawar could do little to save his party.There were moves in 2024 to reunite the two factions of the NCP with both Ajit Pawar and Sharad Pawar striking conciliatory tones. However, the proposed unification talks hit a dead end following Ajit Pawar’s sudden death in January 2026.While both Pawar and Sule have ruled out any rebellion, they would be wary given the recent developments in their state which saw Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena(UBT) suffer a second rebellion.Uddhav lost 6 party MPs to the Eknath Shinde led Shiv Sena four years after the first rebellion by party MLAs that dethroned him from the post of chief minister.Sule reacted sharply to the defections and said: "I honestly wonder if democracy has ceased to exist in this country. Breaking homes and splitting parties has become the norm. These MPs were elected just two and a half years ago, and the next general elections are scheduled for 2029. What was the absolute hurry?"Sule reminded the ruling party of democratic traditions, pointing out that opposition parties like Congress have historically offered unanimous support to the government on crucial legislative matters -- such as the passage of the GST bill -- without requiring political coercion. "What is the need for such vandalism and destruction of parties? If there is no opposition left and everyone joins the government, who will speak for the common citizens?" she asked.The NCP, which is now led by Ajit Pawar’s wife Sunetra Pawar, has just one member in Lok Sabha at present. It may not be averse to increasing its tally for a greater say in the ruling NDA. Both Uddhav Thackeray and Mamata Banerjee had strongly denied reports of any rebellion even as their legislators were plotting a coup. In fact, the other big rebellion this season, which happened in AAP and was the first in the latest series, had also come as a surprise to Arvind Kejriwal and other senior leaders of the party. So, while Sharad Pawar’s confidence is welcome, he needs to be on guard.With NCP MLA Dharmarao Atram setting a December deadline, the fear of defections would be hanging like a sword of Damocles on Sharad Pawar and his daughter Supriya Sule.
Article Summary: Sharad Pawar has asserted that Nationalist Congress Party (SP) will remain united. NCP-SP has at present 8 MPs and 10 MLAs - making the party vulnerable to poaching. Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena(UBT) suffer a second rebellion.

Article Title: 70-year case, older than its judges, finally decided
Article Text:
Representative image

NEW DELHI: A unique land dispute that dragged through the tenures of all past PMs was finally decided by a Supreme Court bench of Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and N V Anjaria - both of whom were not born when the case originated from a 1957 sale deed.The 70-year-old dispute was over 15.5 bighas in Narsipur Kalan village of Haridwar, bought by predecessors of appellant Sarafat Ali through the sale deed of June 4, 1957. SC set aside concurrent findings of trial court and HC and held the deed valid.Justice Mishra succinctly summarised the case: "What initially commenced as proceedings for mutation gradually traversed into the realm of the UP Zamindari Abolition and Land Reforms Act, 1950 and the consolidation framework, though its odyssey across multiple forums only culminated in futility, with the authorities below concurrently holding that the appellants had failed to prove the execution of the said sale deed, thereby compelling them to seek refuge before this court. "Sarafat Ali's predecessors were minors at the time of the deed, which meant it was purchased by their father. During the long winding journey of the case, Sarafat Ali too passed away and the case was fought till SC by his legal heir. Thus, four generations of a family got embroiled in the litigation.At the time of mutation of the land in the name of purchaser, the seller initially objected but later withdrew it to allow revenue authorities to mutate the land in favour of the appellants. When land consolidation proceedings were initiated in the village, the appellants found their name missing as owners of the purchased land, which was still shown in the name of the seller. The consolidation officer, based on the mutation records, recorded the names of appellants as owners of the land.This was challenged by the sellers. The consolidation officer ordered fresh adjudication.
Article Summary: The 70-year-old dispute was over 15.5 bighas in Narsipur Kalan village of Haridwar. Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and N V Anjaria were not born when the case originated from a 1957 sale deed.

Article Title: Congress plan for pre-poll reshuffle in Punjab unit fuels factionalism
Article Text:
Representative image

NEW DELHI: Congress’s decision to take a fresh look at the composition of its Punjab leadership in the run-up to assembly polls seems to have sown confusion rather than settle the prevailing factionalism.The flurry of review meetings and interviews of senior state functionaries has opened the idea of a change of team at the top, with the rank and file again appearing to be divided according to their loyalties.Party functionaries said this was the very development that they were seeking to avoid in the recent months because there is little time for an overhaul of the famously factionalised party branch.Congress formed a committee to look into the Punjab unit and is set to take a decision on its recommendations. The key reason for the final relook at the unit is seemingly its lopsided nature — with both the posts of LoP and state chief with one community (Sikh Jatt) — a result of the hasty appointments done after the 2022 AAP landslide that reduced Congress to just 18 MLAs out of 117.Traditionally, Congress’s organisation has also accommodated Dalit and Hindu faces as a signal to the influential social blocs of Punjab.But, according to insiders, the exercise is too delayed now because the option of changing the state brass could lead to a disconnect with the office bearers down the line who are likely to be loyal to the leadership of the last four years.A change may affect the unity of the ranks, which many believe is what can take Congress over the line in its battle against AAP.However, if the party sticks to the present leadership, it may end up accentuating the perception of social disbalance. “That is why this issue should never have been opened in public,” a member said.
Article Summary: Congress formed a committee to look into the Punjab unit and is set to take a decision on its recommendations. The key reason for the final relook at the unit is seemingly its lopsided nature. Both the posts of LoP and state chief with one community (Sikh Jatt)

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela, over 160 killed; India draws red line on US trade pact & more
Article Text:
Death toll rises to 164 in Venezuela; Piyush Goyal says India cannot enter a deal with the US until the framework of comparative advantage

India will not finalise a trade deal with the US until it secures a comparative tariff advantage over rival exporters. Negotiations are progressing, with both sides aiming for an interim agreement.

Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann denied being the person in a viral sacrilege video, calling it fabricated and politically motivated. He claimed the footage featured an impostor wearing a mask resembling him.

At least 164 people were killed and 971 injured after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela. Authorities declared a state of emergency as rescue teams searched for survivors amid widespread destruction.

The government clarified that an Indian passport is not definitive proof of citizenship, citing the Passports Act. Citizenship is determined under the Citizenship Act through birth, descent, registration, naturalisation, or incorporation.

Ireland advanced the start time of its T20Is against India to avoid overlapping with India's Women's World Cup clash against Australia. The series could also see Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut.

Here are the top 5 stories from the day:

No deal with US without tariff advantage framework, says Piyush Goyal

Bhagwant Mann says 'imposter wearing my mask' for 'sacrilege' video row

Venezuela earthquake: Dual earthquake tremors kill 164; state of emergency declared

MEA says passport not citizenship proof; documentation explained

India vs Ireland: Match timings changed ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's expected debut

Union commerce minister Piyush Goyal said India will not sign the proposed bilateral trade agreement with the US until a framework guaranteeing a comparative tariff advantage for Indian exports is finalised.India and the US have reviewed progress on the interim trade pact, covering market access, digital trade and supply chains. Both countries are working to conclude an agreement before the July 24 deadline for the expiry of Washington's temporary 10 per cent tariff regime. Read the full story Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann rejected allegations linking him to a viral sacrilege video, asserting that the person seen was an impostor wearing a mask resembling him.Citing the absence of a surgery scar on the individual's neck, Mann called the video fake and part of a political conspiracy to damage his image ahead of the 2027 Punjab assembly elections. He also questioned the Akal Takht's actions and announced plans to submit evidence for scrutiny. Read the full story Two powerful earthquakes measuring 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude struck Venezuela, killing at least 164 people and injuring 971 others. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez declared a nationwide state of emergency as rescue operations continued in heavily affected areas, particularly La Guaira state.Buildings collapsed, critical infrastructure was damaged, and Caracas' main international airport was shut down. Authorities warned the death toll could rise as search efforts continue. Read the full story The government clarified that while a passport certifies nationality abroad, it is not conclusive proof of citizenship. Citing Section 20 of the Passports Act, 1967, officials noted that passports can, in certain circumstances, be issued to non-citizens in the public interest.Citizenship is determined under the Citizenship Act, 1955, through birth, descent, registration, naturalisation, or incorporation of territory, with supporting documents serving as evidence. Read the full story Cricket Ireland has moved the start time of its two T20Is against India to 6 pm IST, an hour earlier than usual, to avoid a clash with India's Women's World Cup match against Australia.The Belfast series, beginning Friday, could mark the international debut of 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Shreyas Iyer's first stint as India's T20 captain. Former India leg-spinner MV Narsimha Rao will ring the ceremonial bell before the second match. Read the full story
Article Summary: India will not finalise a trade deal with the US until it secures a comparative tariff advantage over rival exporters. Bhagwant Mann denied being the person in a viral sacrilege video, calling it fabricated and politically motivated. At least 164 people were killed and 971 injured after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela. Ireland advanced the start time of its T20Is against India to avoid overlapping with India's Women's World Cup clash against Australia.

Article Title: Sunil Chhetri applauds Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo's unending hunger at World Cup 2026
Article Text:
Sunil Chhetri, a legendary name in Indian football, celebrated the insatiable ambition and sportsmanship of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, both still shining bright in their late thirties. (Photo/ Agency)

Record-Breaking Icons

Standout Moments

Indian football icon Sunil Chhetri has showered rich praise on Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo , urging young athletes to replicate their legendary longevity and undying competitive fire.Speaking as a pre-show analyst on Zee5's expert panel for the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026, the former Indian captain expressed his amazement at how both superstars continue to dominate the sport well into their late thirties."Think about Messi and Ronaldo, who are almost 40. For them to perform like this on the world stage is unbelievable," Chhetri stated. "If there is one thing young footballers should learn from them, it's their hunger. They have won almost everything in football, but they still play with the same desire and commitment."Both veterans have set the tournament ablaze with historic milestones. Argentina's Lionel Messi rewrote the record books to become the World Cup's all-time men’s leading goalscorer. His clinical brace during a 2-0 group-stage win over Austria took his tally to 18 goals, overtaking Germany's Miroslav Klose who previously held the record at 16.Meanwhile, Cristiano Ronaldo achieved immortality by becoming the first player to score in six different World Cups. The Portuguese talisman netted twice in a 5-0 demolition of Uzbekistan. At 41 years and 138 days, Ronaldo also cemented his place as the second-oldest goalscorer in World Cup history, trailing only Cameroon legend Roger Milla.When asked about his personal highlights of the mega-event so far, Chhetri highlighted Messi’s vintage hat-trick and Japan’s statement 4-0 victory. "Japan’s win was a special moment because it showcased how far Asian football has progressed," Chhetri added, while also recalling the electrifying atmosphere of the Morocco-Scotland match he witnessed live in Boston.The tournament has also seen the next generation rise to the occasion. France's Kylian Mbappé hit a century of international appearances and joined Klose on 16 World Cup goals, while Norway's Erling Haaland became the first player since 2018 to score multiple goals in his opening two games, racking up four goals in Group I.Chhetri feels fans should stop comparing the eras and simply cherish the spectacles. "Messi and Ronaldo have created their own legacy, while Mbappé and Haaland will create their own stories. As a neutral, just watch and enjoy," he concluded.
Article Summary: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are both still shining bright in their late thirties. Chhetri urges young athletes to replicate their legendary longevity and undying competitive fire. The former Indian captain feels fans should stop comparing the eras and simply cherish the spectacles.

Article Title: Japan vs Sweden, FIFA World Cup match result: Japan, Sweden play out 1-1 draw to qualify for Round of 32
Article Text:
AP Photo

Japan and Sweden shared the points in a 1-1 draw on Thursday, with both teams securing places in the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup The result saw Japan finish second in Group F behind the Netherlands, while Sweden also advanced to the Round of 32. Japan will now face tournament favourites Brazil on Monday, while Sweden's next opponents will be confirmed after the remaining group-stage matches.After a quiet first half, the game came alive in the second period. Japan struck first in the 56th minute when Ritsu Doan played a perfectly weighted pass to Daizen Maeda, who controlled the ball before calmly finishing past goalkeeper Jacob Widell Zetterstrom.The goal was Japan's seventh of the tournament, the country's highest-ever tally in a single World Cup, surpassing the six goals they scored during their run to the Round of 16 in 2018.Sweden responded quickly. Just six minutes later, Anthony Elanga fired a superb left-footed strike from outside the penalty area that beat Japan goalkeeper Zion Suzuki to level the scores. It was Elanga's second goal of the tournament.Both teams had chances to find a winner. Alexander Isak came close for Sweden, but Suzuki produced an excellent save before later tipping another header onto the crossbar in stoppage time and gathering the loose ball.Sweden also earned eight corner kicks compared to Japan's two, but the Japanese defence stood firm to ensure they left with the point they needed.The draw means Japan have reached the World Cup knockout stage for the third consecutive edition and for the fifth time in the last seven tournaments since first making the Round of 16 on home soil in 2002.Sweden, meanwhile, continued their impressive World Cup record, progressing beyond the group stage for the fourth consecutive time they have qualified for the tournament, a run stretching back to 1994.
Article Summary: Japan and Sweden draw 1-1 in Group F. Both teams progress to the Round of 32. Japan will now face tournament favourites Brazil on Monday. Sweden's next opponents will be confirmed after remaining group-stage matches. Japan have reached the World Cup knockout stage for the third consecutive edition and for the fifth time in the last seven tournaments.

Article Title: USA vs Turkey, FIFA World Cup match result: Kaan Ayhan shines as Turkey edge USA 3-2 with dramatic last-gasp winner despite early elimination
Article Text:
Image credit: FIFA World Cup

USA strike early, Turkey hit back

Berhalter levels, Pulisic returns

Ayhan seals dramatic win

USA march on despite defeat

Turkey signed off from the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 3-2 victory over the United States, scoring the winning goal with virtually the final kick of the match on Thursday.Kaan Ayhan was the hero for Turkey, finding the net deep into stoppage time after Can Uzun squared the ball across the goal. Although Turkey had already been knocked out after losing their first two group matches, they ended their campaign on a high with an entertaining win over the already-qualified hosts.The United States had secured top spot in Group D before kickoff with wins over Paraguay and Australia. Mauricio Pochettino rested several regular starters, making nine changes to the lineup, with Christian Pulisic returning from injury as a second-half substitute.The hosts made the perfect start when defender Auston Trusty scored in just the third minute to send the home fans into celebration.Turkey responded quickly through Real Madrid youngster Arda Guler, who finished off a fine move in the 10th minute to level the score. Guler then turned provider just before half-time, helping create the move that ended with Orkun Kokcu putting Turkey 2-1 ahead.The United States fought back soon after the restart. Sebastian Berhalter struck from outside the box in the 49th minute to make it 2-2.Pulisic returned to action in the 58th minute after recovering from a calf injury that had kept him out since the opening match. The AC Milan midfielder made an immediate impact, creating chances with his direct running and came close to scoring himself. His best effort struck the post, while Brenden Aaronson failed to convert the rebound.Just when the game looked set to end in a draw, Turkey found one final attack in the eighth minute of stoppage time. Can Uzun collected the ball at the far post and squared it to Ayhan, who slid in to score the dramatic winner.It was Turkey's only victory of the tournament after defeats in their opening two group matches.The match also produced another tournament milestone. Trusty's early goal was the United States' seventh of the World Cup, matching the country's highest group-stage scoring tally. It also became the 173rd goal of the tournament, setting a new record for the most goals scored at a single World Cup before the knockout stage.Despite the defeat, the United States progress to the Round of 32 as Group D winners and will face Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday. Turkey, meanwhile, bow out with a morale-boosting victory after an otherwise disappointing campaign.
Article Summary: Turkey beat the United States 3-2 in their final World Cup group match. Auston Trusty and Arda Guler put the hosts ahead before Orkun Kokcu equalized. Sebastian Berhalter made it 2-2 beforeaan Ayhan scored the winner in stoppage time.

Article Title: Netherlands vs Tunisia, FIFA World Cup match result: Netherlands beat Tunisia to enter Round of 32
Article Text:
AP Photo

The Netherlands sealed top spot in Group F with a comfortable 3-1 win over Tunisia on Thursday, setting up a Round of 32 clash against Morocco at the FIFA World Cup Ronald Koeman's side finished ahead of Japan, who were held to a draw by Sweden earlier in the day. The result meant the Dutch advanced as group winners, while Japan will face tournament favourites Brazil in the knockout stage.The Netherlands made the perfect start in rainy conditions at Kansas City Stadium, taking the lead within two minutes. Tunisia captain Ellyes Skhiri accidentally turned Denzel Dumfries' cross into his own net while trying to stop Brian Brobbey from getting on the end of it.Five minutes later, Brobbey doubled the lead with a close-range finish after Virgil van Dijk headed a free-kick back across goal. It was the striker's third goal of the tournament.The early blitz also created history, with the Netherlands scoring twice inside the opening seven minutes of a World Cup match for the first time.Tunisia, who had already been eliminated after losing their first two matches and changing coaches during the tournament, struggled to respond. Ismael Gharbi had an early chance before the Dutch took control, but he failed to hit the target.The African side found hope midway through the second half when Hazem Mastouri headed home from Hannibal Mejbri's corner to reduce the deficit to 2-1.However, the Dutch restored their two-goal advantage almost immediately. Defender Jan Paul van Hecke scored his first international goal, with his header from Tijjani Reijnders' corner taking a slight deflection off Anis Slimane before ending up in the net.The result completed an impressive group-stage campaign for the Netherlands, who now head into the knockout rounds full of confidence.Their attention now turns to Monday's Round of 32 meeting with Morocco, while Tunisia bow out of the tournament without adding to their solitary point.
Article Summary: Netherlands beat Tunisia 3-1 to finish top of Group F. Ronald Koeman's side will now face Morocco in the Round of 32. Japan were held to a draw by Sweden earlier in the day. Japan will face tournament favourites Brazil in the knockout stage.

Article Title: Lucknow building fire: Artist’s anniversary sketch for parents reaches them after his death
Article Text:
Nilesh was among the 15 people killed in the devastating Aliganj fire. In the days that followed, as Nilesh’s grief-stricken family gathered his belongings, they came across the carefully preserved sketch.

Poll Vote & Share your view Should schools include art therapy programs to support emotional expression? Yes, they can be very beneficial No, traditional subjects are more important 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion





LUCKNOW: For weeks, 24-year-old 3D artist Nilesh Kumar had quietly worked on a surprise he never got to deliver. Tucked away inside a notebook in his room was a hand-drawn sketch of his parents, Shatruhan Lal (70) and Santosh Kumari (60), he had planned to gift them on their wedding anniversary in July.The prized souvenir reached the distraught parents only after they had lit the son’s pyre.Nilesh was among the 15 people killed in the devastating Aliganj fire. In the days that followed, as Nilesh’s grief-stricken family gathered his belongings, they came across the carefully preserved sketch.The portrait, intended to celebrate his parents’ lifelong companionship, has now become the family’s most cherished possession, and its deepest heartbreak.Santosh Kumari sits for hours holding the drawing against her chest, running her trembling fingers over the pencil lines her son so lovingly drew. Shatruhan Lal, who retired from the electricity department, stays quiet for most of the day.“He wanted to hand it to them himself,” Nilesh’s elder brother Abhishek Kumar told TOI.Family members said Nilesh intended to present the portrait around Guru Purnima on July 29, when the family would have celebrated his parents’ wedding anniversary.What was meant to be a moment of laughter, surprise and photographs together has instead become a memory marked by silence.Every stroke of the sketch reflects the young artist’s affection for his parents. While the portrait captures their smiling faces, the couple who inspired it now look at it through tears.Drawing was not merely Nilesh’s profession; it was the language through which he expressed himself. Abhishek shared that Nilesh took to sketching when he was 13 or 14 years old, effortlessly recreating popular cartoon characters such as Motu Patlu, Chhota Bheem and Shinchan.“By the time he was 16, he decided to make a career in animation,” Abhishek said.After completing professional training in animation, Nilesh spent the past three to four years establishing himself as a 3D artist, earning appreciation for his creativity while dreaming of giving his parents a better life.The brothers’ last conversation now haunts the family. “He was checking flight tickets to Kolkata for Durga Puja in October. We chatted about travel plans, family and other things. It was a normal conversation. We never imagined those would be the last words we would ever exchange.”The family has received a compensation cheque of Rs 5 lakh from govt, while the remaining assistance is still under process. “Everything happened so suddenly,” Abhishek said. “People came to help, but we were not in a state to understand anything.”For the family, the sketch is no longer a piece of artwork. It is Nilesh’s final message of love, one that arrived too late. The anniversary gift that was meant to bring smiles to two ageing parents has instead become the last trace of the son who dreamt of making them happy, even in the smallest of ways.
Article Summary: Nilesh Kumar, 24, was among the 15 people killed in the devastating Aliganj fire. Tucked away inside a notebook in his room was a hand-drawn sketch of his parents. The prized souvenir reached the distraught parents only after they had lit the son’s pyre.

Article Title: Women's T20 World Cup: Shafali Verma stars as India beat Bangladesh to stay in semi-final race
Article Text:
Shafali Verma (BCCI Women Photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think India fielding lapses will impact their chances against Australia? Yes, it will definitely impact. No, they can recover. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

England storm into semis

The good news is that India are still in the hunt for a semifinal berth in the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup with a five-wicket victory over Bangladesh in Manchester on Thursday. But they probably got lucky on the day. A lacklustre performance like this could prove costly against Australia, who they face on Sunday in their last league game.Four dropped catches inside the Powerplay and 10 extras, including seven wides, don’t make for attributes of a clinical side. The reigning ODI champions were unbearably sloppy on the field.Spinner Shree Charani (2/21) bore the brunt of her teammates’ lapses, with three catches going down off her bowling. Juairiya Ferdous was handed three reprieves, one each by Yastika Bhatia, Radha Yadav and Nandani Sharma. Nandani later also dropped Sobhana Mostary. Additionally, Nandani was warned twice for running on the pitch.The dropped catches allowed Bangladesh to post 136/8.Radha, who replaced Prema Rawat in the playing XI, made amends for the dropped catch by claiming three wickets. Renuka Singh, brought in for Arundhati Reddy, provided the first breakthrough when Dilara Akter pulled a short ball straight to Shree Charani in the deep.Juairiya (33), Sobhana Mostary (22) and captain Nigar Sultana (32) all got starts but failed to convert them. India’s chase began in a contrasting fashion. Smriti Mandhana (8) departed in the third over. Shafali Verma, who survived a dropped chance in the opening over, responded with an innings packed with power and backed by placement.Her 53-ball 34 set the tone with a flurry of boundaries and topped it with a massive six off Shanjida Akter over the long-on boundary. Her innings ended against the run of play when she lost balance and wicketkeeper Sultana was quick with the stumping off Nahida Akter’s bowling. However, the middle-order’s familiar inconsistencies resurfaced as Yastika Bhatia and Richa Ghosh departed in successive overs. Jemimah Rodrigues made 26. Deepti Sharma finally completed the chase with a boundary.England became the first into the semifinals of the tournament as they beat the West Indies by 38 runs at Lord’s on Wednesday.Brief scores: India 139/5 in 16.5 overs (S Verma 53, J Rodrigues 26; R Moni 2/29) beat Bangladesh: 136/8 (J Ferdous 33; R Yadav 3/28, S Charani 2/21)
Article Summary: India beat Bangladesh by five wickets in ICC Women's T20 World Cup quarter- final. Shafali Verma scored 53 not out as India reached 139-5 in 16.5 overs. England became the first into the semifinals of the tournament as they beat the West Indies by 38 runs at Lord's on Wednesday.

Article Title: 6,6,6,6,0,6! Marcus Stoinis goes berserk in Ian Holland's 17th over for Seattle Orcas - Watch
Article Text:
Marcus Stoinis (Image: X)

Seattle Orcas crush Washington Freedom by 88 runs

Poll Vote & Share your view How would you rate the performance of the Washington Freedom batting lineup? Excellent Average 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Marcus Stoinis unleashed a stunning display of power-hitting during the 17th over, smashing five sixes off Washington Freedom seamer Ian Holland to turn the match decisively in Seattle Orcas' favour in their MLC 2026 clash at the Oakland Coliseum.The Australian all-rounder had made a slow start, managing just 4 runs from his first six deliveries, but he flipped the script in spectacular fashion. Taking full advantage of the death overs, Stoinis tore into Holland, clearing the boundary five times in the over to collect 30 runs and shift the momentum completely.He wasn't done there. In the following over, Stoinis launched Marco Jansen for another towering six before being caught on the very next ball. His explosive cameo of 42 off just 16 deliveries, featuring six sixes, came at a remarkable strike rate of 262.50.Asked to bat first, the Seattle Orcas overcame a steady start with a spectacular display of power-hitting led by Shimron Hetmyer. The West Indies batter lit up the Oakland Coliseum with a blistering 79 off 33 balls, smashing nine sixes and two fours to put the Freedom bowlers under immense pressure.He received excellent support from skipper Marcus Stoinis, who produced a whirlwind 42 off just 16 deliveries, including six sixes, while opener Tim Seifert laid the platform with a brisk 37 from 20 balls.Although Marco Jansen impressed with figures of 3/33, Seattle finished on a daunting 227/6, leaving Washington Freedom with a stiff chase.The reply never gained momentum as Jasdeep Singh delivered a devastating spell with the new ball. The right-arm seamer tore through the top order, finishing with outstanding figures of 5/24 in four overs to put the Freedom on the back foot from the outset.Washington's star-studded batting lineup crumbled under pressure, with Steven Smith (1), Glenn Maxwell (0) and Mitchell Owen (5) all falling cheaply as the side slumped to 50/7 inside the first eight overs.A late counterattack from Amila Aponso, who struck 31 off 13 balls, and Ian Holland (26) added some respectability to the score, but the outcome was never in doubt. Cameron Gannon chipped in with 2/16 as Washington Freedom were eventually bowled out for 139 in 16.2 overs, handing Seattle Orcas a commanding 88-run victory.
Article Summary: Seattle Orcas crush Washington Freedom by 88 runs in MLC 2026 clash. Shimron Hetmyer hit 79 off 33 balls, including nine sixes and two fours. Marcus Stoinis produced a whirlwind 42 off just 16 deliveries, including six sixes. Jasdeep Singh delivered a devastating spell with the new ball, taking 5/24.

Article Title: How to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite
Article Text:
Image via Fortnite

How to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite



Is there a guaranteed way to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite?



What does the Grim Reaper Sprite do in Fortnite?



With five brand new Sprites arriving in Fortnite's v41.10 update, the Shattered Coast is filled with more content when it comes to Sprite hunting. Among all the newcomers introduced as part of the Fortnite Gone Wild event, the Grim Reaper Sprite has immediately established itself as one of the most coveted.It is also among the most difficult ones to add to your collection, making it a hot topic within the Fortnite community. If you are looking for more details about finding it, you are at the right place.The section below guides you on obtaining the Grim Reaper Sprite.The Grim Reaper Sprite is a Mythic Sprite, so it’s very rare and hard to get in Fortnite. There’s no guaranteed way to get it, and no location on the map where it reliably spawns, so it’s a true test of patience and persistence.Speaking of where to find it, the Grim Reaper Sprite can be found in Sprite Chests, regular chests, rare chests, golden fishing spots, and as floor loot around the Fortnite island. Of these methods, Sprite Chests are still the best and most reliable method as they’re the only source guaranteed to have at least one Sprite in each opening.Your best bet for finding a Grim Reaper Sprite is opening up as many Sprite Chests as you can find.Vaults are great spots to visit for them, as these treasure troves usually have at least two Sprite Chests available to open. Prioritising Vault locations at the start of a match is one of the most efficient strategies for grinding rare Sprite drops.Fishing out of gold pools around the island is another great method. These drop a variety of loot, and do not always contain Sprites, but when they do, they are oftentimes really rare and unique ones.The answer to this question is no, as the Grim Sprite does not have a guaranteed spawn location for the Grim Sprite in Fortnite. Instead, it is a random loot drop that can appear anywhere around the island, which means luck and random chance play a massive role in how easy or hard it is to find.If finding one through natural loot is proving frustratingly tiring, keep an eye out for players willing to trade. The Grim Reaper Sprite's utility in combat makes it one of the most valuable trade commodities currently in Chapter 7 Season 3.When equipped, the Grim Reaper Sprite marks anyone who attacks you for a short period of time. This makes it an outstanding combat tool, i.e., if an opponent lands a hit on you and tries to disappear into cover or flee across the map, the mark reveals their position, making it significantly harder for them to escape follow-up damage. For aggressive players who frequently find themselves in high-stakes duels, the Grim Reaper Sprite is one of the most impactful Mythic Sprites currently in the game.This was all you needed to know about the Grim Reaper Sprite.
Article Summary: The Grim Reaper Sprite is a Mythic Sprite, so it’s very rare and hard to get in Fortnite. There's no guaranteed way to get it, and no location on the map where it reliably spawns. It can be found in Sprite Chests, regular chests, rare chests, golden fishing spots, and as floor loot around the island.

Article Title: What LaMelo Ball’s departure means for Charlotte Hornets? Roster outlook and more
Article Text:
Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball. Image via: David Berding/Getty Images

What LaMelo Ball's departure means for Charlotte Hornets

Who won in the LaMelo Ball trade ?

Minnesota Timberwolves receive Charlotte Hornets receive LaMelo Ball Naz Reid Josh Green 2033 first-round pick (unprotected) 2028 first-round pick swap 2029 first-round pick swap 2030 first-round pick swap 2029 second-round pick 2032 second-round pick 2033 second-round pick

Charlotte Hornets' new roster outlook

LaMelo Ball's departure signals the start of a new chapter for the Charlotte Hornets after the franchise agreed to trade its former All-Star point guard to the Minnesota Timberwolves. The blockbuster deal, reported Thursday, sends Ball and Josh Green to Minnesota in exchange for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick, three first-round pick swaps and three second-round selections.The trade hints at Charlotte's long-term direction. Rather than continuing to build around Ball, the Hornets are committing to a younger core led by Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel while gaining financial flexibility and valuable draft assets that could fuel additional moves this offseason.Charlotte's decision represents more than a roster change. It reflects a philosophical shift under president of basketball operations Jeff Peterson and head coach Charles Lee.Ball spent six seasons as the face of the franchise, but league executives increasingly believed Charlotte had concerns about his durability, long-term salary commitment and overall fit within its evolving roster. Those concerns ultimately resulted in one of the biggest trades of the NBA offseason.The Hornets wasted little time adjusting to life without their franchise guard, agreeing to a three-year, $74 million extension with Coby White. The move positions White as the team's new starting point guard while giving Charlotte another dependable scorer alongside Miller and Knueppel.Financially, the Hornets are now in one of the NBA's strongest positions. Reports indicate they sit roughly $75 million below the luxury tax while also creating a record-setting $40.8 million trade exception. That combination gives Charlotte significant flexibility to pursue another impact player or absorb contracts in future trades.Here’s the complete look of the deal, as per CBS The deal has already sparked strong opinions across the league. FS1 personality Nick Wright praised Charlotte's approach.“Congratulations to the Charlotte Hornets for making a tough decision when they needed to make it… I don’t understand what Minnesota is doing, even if you like the talent more than I do in LaMelo Ball,” Wright said.Minnesota clearly views the transaction differently. Pairing Ball with Anthony Edwards gives the Timberwolves another elite playmaker capable of easing Edwards' offensive workload while creating one of the NBA's most dynamic backcourts.From Charlotte's perspective, the trade is as much about future flexibility as immediate talent. Naz Reid brings floor spacing and frontcourt versatility, while the collection of draft assets gives the organization multiple pathways to continue reshaping its roster.Charlotte's projected lineup now features Coby White, Kon Knueppel, Brandon Miller, Naz Reid and Moussa Diabate, though additional moves appear likely.Miles Bridges continues to surface in trade discussions as he enters the final year of his contract, while reports suggest Charlotte possesses enough cap flexibility to pursue another established star if the right opportunity emerges. Jaylen Brown has been floated as one possible target in league speculation, though no deal appears imminent.For now, the Hornets are placing their future in the hands of Miller and Knueppel. Both flourished alongside Ball, but the responsibility now shifts to them to lead a franchise betting that a deeper, more flexible roster can produce more sustained success than the previous era.
Article Summary: LaMelo Ball's departure signals the start of a new chapter for the Charlotte Hornets. The franchise agreed to trade its former All-Star point guard to the Minnesota Timberwolves. The blockbuster deal, reported Thursday, sends Ball and Josh Green to Minnesota in exchange for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick.

Article Title: “This is beyond disappointing”: Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video raises eyebrows while kidnapping case takes dramatic turn
Article Text:
Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video raises eyebrows while kidnapping case takes dramatic turn(Getty)

Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video sparks reactions while details of the Tampa incident stay in focus

Terrion Arnold’s kidnapping case grows more serious as co-defendants flip and the bond gets denied



Terrion Arnold’s arrest has quickly become one of the biggest NFL stories of the offseason. The Detroit Lions cornerback surrendered to Florida authorities after being charged in connection with a February kidnapping and armed robbery case in Tampa, putting both his freedom and football future in serious doubt.Now, Terrion Arnold’s court appearance has pushed the case into a new spotlight after leaked courtroom footage sparked strong reactions online, while fresh legal developments gave prosecutors even more momentum ahead of Monday’s hearing.The leaked courtroom video became the first thing people noticed Thursday. Terrion Arnold stood before a Hillsborough County judge wearing a green anti-suicide safety vest, often used inside county jails for inmates under closer supervision. That visual quickly became the center of online discussion.Social media is filled with questions. One fan asked, “Why did they get him in a turtle suit?” Another wrote, “This got to be a ritual.”Another said, “Damn, suicide watch!?!? They have camp in like 5 weeks!!!!”Another added, “It’s a Detroit thing 😭 toxic out here.”One folk added, “This is beyond disappointing on all levels. Gotta be careful with folks you deal with”.According to investigators, the Terrion Arnold arrest connects back to a February incident in Tampa. Prosecutors allege Arnold helped orchestrate a plan where three men were lured into an apartment, then robbed at gunpoint and held against their will. Authorities later filed four kidnapping charges and four armed robbery charges against him, making this one of the most serious criminal cases involving an active NFL player this year.For many following the case, the leaked video only pushed more attention toward an investigation that is still unfolding.Terrion Arnold’s first court hearing brought no relief. The judge denied bond on June 25 and ordered him to remain in custody through the weekend. His next court appearance is set for Monday, where his legal team will again argue for release.But what makes that hearing even more important is what happened earlier the same day. Two co-defendants tied to the same case pleaded guilty in Hillsborough County and agreed to cooperate with prosecutors. Both are now expected to testify against Terrion Arnold, a major shift that strengthens the state’s case.That development has already changed public reaction. One user wrote, “His circle is trash,” blaming the people around Arnold for dragging him deeper into the case. Another comment suggested Arnold’s friends may have turned on him to reduce their own sentences.Arnold’s defence team is pushing back hard. His agent, Denise White of EAG Sports Management, released a statement saying Terrion Arnold “categorically denies” any involvement and insisted prosecutors are relying on convicted felons trying to cut deals.Support has also come from outside his camp. Sauce Gardner publicly defended Arnold, calling him a “good kid” and saying the allegations do not match the person he knows.For the Detroit Lions, this situation creates major uncertainty. Terrion Arnold was expected to be a key part of Detroit’s secondary this season. Now, with the Terrion Arnold arrest case moving quickly and witness testimony building, Monday’s hearing could become the biggest turning point yet.
Article Summary: Terrion Arnold’s kidnapping case grows more serious as co-defendants flip and the bond gets denied. The Detroit Lions cornerback surrendered to Florida authorities after being charged in connection with a February kidnapping and armed robbery case in Tampa. The leaked courtroom video became the first thing people noticed Thursday.

Article Title: NBA insider drops bad news on longest tenured Knicks star weeks after championship victory
Article Text:
Mitchell Robinson. Image via: Jesse D. Garrabrant /NBAE via Getty Images

NBA insider reveals Mitchell Robinson's future with the Knicks



Why the Knicks may struggle to keep their championship core together



Mitchell Robinson. Image via: Emilee Chinn/Getty Images

The New York Knicks could be headed for a difficult offseason decision after an NBA insider cast fresh doubt over Mitchell Robinson's future with the franchise. Just weeks after winning the 2026 NBA championship, multiple reports now suggest the Knicks are unlikely to retain their longest-tenured player because of looming salary cap restrictions.The uncertainty centers on New York's determination to remain below the NBA's second luxury tax apron. That financial approach could force the front office to break up key pieces of a championship roster despite Robinson's importance on both ends of the floor.Mitchell Robinson enters free agency after completing the four-year, $60 million contract he signed in 2022. While appearing on ESPN's Unsportsmanlike radio show, Brian Windhorst said Robinson's return remains far from certain.“He’s a bit of a polarizing player because he’s had some health issues and he hasn’t been able to play starter’s minutes, so do you give him starter money?" Windhorst said.“So, I would say Mitch Robinson is 50/50. I don’t know, maybe 51-49 to come back. But that’s open that he might not be there.”That assessment followed SNY's Ian Begley reporting there are "several teams with big-time interest" in Robinson, with at least one organization potentially offering him a starting role.Veteran Knicks reporter Stefan Bondy echoed the growing expectation, posting, "Heard it’s unlikely Mitchell Robinson will be back with Knicks next season."The combination of outside interest and New York's financial limits has turned Robinson into one of the franchise's biggest offseason questions.The Knicks entered the draft focused on creating financial flexibility, but keeping Robinson may still prove difficult.ESPN's Shams Charania reported that Robinson and guard Landry Shamet are not expected to return as unrestricted free agents because owner James Dolan is unwilling to exceed the second apron. Salary cap projections indicate New York has limited room to re-sign both players while filling out the remainder of its roster.Robinson's value further complicates negotiations. Although injuries have prevented him from consistently handling starter-level minutes, he remains one of the NBA's premier offensive rebounders. Last season, he posted a 23.9 percent offensive rebounding rate, surpassing the previous league record held by Steven Adams.Analysts Fred Katz and Sam Vecenie recently projected Robinson could command roughly two years and $30 million on the open market, while estimates around the league suggest the Knicks are unlikely to exceed $15 million annually if they remain committed to staying below the second apron.Robinson averaged 4.8 points and 5.9 rebounds in 14 minutes per game during the championship season after the Knicks carefully managed his workload throughout the year. His rim protection and rebounding became critical during the playoff run, making any departure difficult to replace.New York now faces a defining offseason choice. It can preserve financial flexibility or spend beyond the second apron to retain one of the longest-serving and most respected players in the organization, a decision that could reshape the roster before the franchise begins defending its NBA title.
Article Summary: NBA insider casts fresh doubt over Mitchell Robinson's future with the New York Knicks. Multiple reports suggest the Knicks are unlikely to retain their longest-tenured player because of looming salary cap restrictions. The uncertainty centers on New York's determination to remain below the NBA's second luxury tax apron.

Article Title: Doug Martin cause of death: Fresh lawsuit reveals troubling new details about former NFL star’s final hours
Article Text:
Doug Martin cause of death: Fresh lawsuit reveals troubling new details about former NFL star’s final hours (Image via Getty)

Doug Martin's cause of death: Ex-NFL star's parents claim police restraint played a role in his death

Doug Martin’s family wants answers as his football legacy meets a painful final chapter

Former NFL running back Doug Martin’s death shocked the football world when news broke that he had died at 36 last October. Now, a fresh lawsuit has brought troubling new details into focus as Doug Martin’s parents are pushing for answers about his final moments.Recent reports say the lawsuit targets the City of Oakland, the Oakland Police Department, and ambulance provider Falck Northern California. The family claims Doug Martin’s final moments involved police restraint and delayed emergency care during what they describe as a mental health crisis.According to recent reports, Doug Martin’s mother called for medical help after the former Tampa Bay Buccaneers star began struggling during a mental health episode at home. Before paramedics arrived, Doug Martin reportedly left the house and entered a nearby home, where police later found him.That encounter now sits at the center of the lawsuit. The complaint says officers restrained Doug Martin face down while pressing on his back. The family claims that after officers turned him over, Doug Martin showed no response. Reports say officers initially believed he was pretending to sleep before realizing the situation had turned critical.Per Associated Press, the lawsuit also alleges Doug Martin did not receive timely medical care after becoming unresponsive. His parents claim paramedics arrived more than 15 minutes after officers requested emergency assistance and did not begin treatment immediately after reaching the scene. Alongside the police restraint allegations, the family argues that the delayed medical response was a major factor in Doug Martin’s death.Family attorney John Burris told The Athletic, while discussing the lawsuit, “They just want to know what happened.”He also added, “When you call for help, and the police come, it’s not a death warrant.”Those remarks now sit at the heart of this legal fight. The family believes Doug Martin died from restraint asphyxia, which means pressure during the restraint may have cut off his breathing. An independent pathologist reportedly shared that early opinion, though the official findings are still pending.The lawsuit is not just about damages. Doug Martin’s parents are also looking for the full autopsy and toxicology reports, which have still not been released. Recent reports say the coroner’s investigation remains open.At the same time, Doug Martin’s mother has sent his brain to the Boston University CTE Center for testing. The family wants to know if repeated football hits played a role in his mental state before his death.Doug Martin built a strong NFL career after the Tampa Bay Buccaneers drafted him in the first round. He rushed for over 5,300 yards, scored 30 touchdowns, made two Pro Bowls, and earned First-Team All-Pro honors. Doug Martin also spent his final NFL season with the Las Vegas Raiders.For NFL fans, Doug Martin was one of the league’s most explosive backs at his peak. But right now, his family’s fight has shifted the focus away from football and toward the unanswered questions surrounding Doug Martin’s death.
Article Summary: Doug Martin’s parents have filed a lawsuit against the City of Oakland, the Oakland Police Department, and ambulance provider Falck Northern California. The family claims police restraint and delayed emergency care played a role in the death of the former NFL star. Doug Martin died at 36 last October after a mental health episode.

Article Title: “She should be banned indefinitely”: Furious Caitlin Clark fans slam WNBA’s soft punishment to Alyssa Thomas for hitting Fever star’s throat
Article Text:
Alyssa Thomas tries to regain her footing over Caitlin Clark. Image via: Grace Smith / IndyStar / USA Today Network via Imagn

WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas one game after hit to Caitlin Clark's throat



Caitlin Clark. Image via: Grace Smith/IndyStar / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images

Fever fans are fuming over WNBA's soft punishment of Alyssa Thomas for hitting Caitlin Clark's throat



What Fever coach Stephanie White said about the incident



WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas one game after hit to Caitlin Clark's throat, but the league's disciplinary decision has done little to quiet the debate surrounding player safety. The WNBA announced Thursday that Thomas received a retroactive Flagrant Foul 2 and a one-game suspension after making contact with Clark's throat during Phoenix's 111-109 victory over the Indiana Fever.The punishment answers one question about the league's response, but it has created another. Many Fever supporters believe the suspension is too lenient after officials failed to call a foul during the game, allowing a controversial sequence to unfold without an immediate penalty.The play occurred with 6:52 remaining in the second quarter as Clark and Thomas both dove for a loose ball. Video review showed Thomas making contact with her fist to Clark's throat in what the league later ruled was a non-basketball act.No whistle was blown during live action. Following its review, the WNBA upgraded the play and announced disciplinary action."Per WNBA rule, the League Office has the option, following its review of any game, to reclassify a Flagrant foul or to classify as Flagrant any foul not called as such during a game and may impose a fine and/or suspension," the league said in its statement.Thomas will serve the suspension Saturday when the Mercury visit the Toronto Tempo.The incident came during an increasingly physical series between Phoenix and Indiana. Clark later exited with a back injury after another sequence in the game and finished with 19 points and eight assists in 20 minutes. The Fever ultimately lost 111-109.The league's decision immediately sparked strong reactions online, with many fans arguing that one game failed to match the seriousness of the play.One fan wrote, "One game. That’s it 😡. She should be banned indefinitely."Another added, "Hell of a precedent set with only a 1 game suspension," while a third posted, "What a joke. One game. Needs to kicked out of league."Some responses went even further. "Thomas should be expelled from the WNBA!" one user wrote, while another commented, "That’s weak. She should be investigated for assault. It’s clear as day assault with intent."Others took a more measured view. One fan wrote, "Should be longer, but some action taken is better than no action taken."The backlash followed days of heightened discussion surrounding Clark after another heated matchup between the Fever and Mercury, fueling continued conversations about consistency in officiating and player protection.Indiana coach Stephanie White was outspoken after the game, criticizing both the missed call and the standard of officiating.“It was egregious. The fact that it was a no-call … You got to call it,” White said. “You’re coming in here aware of what happened two nights ago and that (expletive) still happens? Absolutely unacceptable.”She later expanded on her frustration.“Absolutely disrespectful. We have a generational talent and a WNBA superstar who had two cheap shots right there that weren’t called," she said. "It's absolutely egregious, and utterly disrespectful. [Clark] is not called the same way as everybody else is called. The fist in the throat is crazy. It's crazy. It's dangerous."The Fever also reinforced that message through team president Kelly Krauskopf.“Player safety should be paramount in our league,” Krauskopf said. “We appreciate the WNBA’s review of last night’s incident and the action taken. Right now our focus is on Caitlin and our entire team as we prepare for Saturday.”With Thomas sidelined for Phoenix's next game and Clark's status still under close watch following her back issue, scrutiny of how the league handles physical play is unlikely to fade anytime soon.
Article Summary: Alyssa Thomas received a retroactive Flagrant Foul 2 and a one-game suspension. Thomas made contact with her fist to Caitlin Clark's throat during Phoenix's 111-109 victory over the Indiana Fever. The league's decision sparked strong reactions online, with many fans arguing that one game failed to match the seriousness of the play.

Article Title: Lakers legend Magic Johnson enjoys vacation with stunning ESPN star Laura Rutledge
Article Text:
Magic Johnson and Laura Rutledge. Image via: Kirby Lee-Imagn Images

Magic Johnson links up with stunning ESPN host Laura Rutledge during scenic vacation



Magic Johnson links up with stunning ESPN host Laura Rutledge. Image via: Laura Rutledge/ magicjohnson/ Instagram

Fans react to Magic Johnson and Laura Rutledge's encounter



Magic Johnson and Laura Rutledge. Image via: magicjohnson/ Instagram

Laura Rutledge drew attention this week after sharing photos from her trip to Cannes, where the ESPN host spent time with NBA Hall of Famer Magic Johnson during the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity. The meeting brought together two of the biggest names in sports media and basketball through a growing business partnership that extends well beyond the playing field.Rutledge's appearance alongside Johnson highlighted the expanding influence both figures have outside their traditional roles. While Johnson continues to grow one of sports' most successful business portfolios, Rutledge is preparing for another milestone in her broadcasting career with ESPN after recently stepping away from SEC Nation.Laura Rutledge documented the France trip on Instagram, sharing a series of photos from Cannes alongside the caption, “Cannes, what a dream!!“So thankful to Disney Advertising for having me!”The connection came through Publicis Connected Media, which announced a formal partnership with Magic Johnson Enterprises in February. Under the agreement, Johnson serves as an advisor while providing access to his network of culturally relevant business and media platforms.Johnson also reflected on the gathering through his own Instagram account after spending time with Rutledge and other executives during the festival.“I had some great meetings today in Cannes with some impressive brands thanks to my partners at Publicis! “I was joined by ESPN reporter Laura Rutledge and 3Arts Sports Co-Founder and CEO Aaron Eanes this afternoon, and we really enjoyed our time and conversations.“Thank you to Publicis Sports CEO Suzy Deering for an awesome day and Publicis Groupe CEO Arthur Sadoun!”The annual Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity has become one of the world's premier gatherings for leaders in advertising, marketing and media. Johnson's presence reflects how his influence has continued to expand since retiring from basketball. The five-time NBA champion now holds ownership interests in the Los Angeles Dodgers, LAFC, the Washington Commanders, the Los Angeles Sparks, the Washington Spirit and esports organization Team Liquid.Rutledge is also entering a significant new chapter professionally. Although she concluded a decade-long run as host of SEC Nation in May, she remains ESPN's Monday Night Football sideline reporter and host of NFL Live. She is also expected to become part of ESPN's first Super Bowl broadcast team when the network airs Super Bowl LXI in February 2027.The social media posts quickly generated thousands of reactions, with many followers praising both the business collaboration and the picturesque setting in Cannes.One fan wrote, "I love your vacation pics 😍," while another commented, "Magic smart business moves........now he gets to write off part of his lavish luxury vacation as business travel😂😂😂😂😂." Others focused on Johnson's growing influence outside basketball, with one user posting, "OMG! I love @publix dawg. They invested here early. 🙏❤️🇺🇸"The meeting also prompted lighter reactions. "Can I get in on this TimeShare member meeting 🙏👌🫡😂," one follower joked, while another simply added, "God bless 🙏."Although the photos captured a relaxed moment in southern France, the gathering also reflected the increasingly close relationship between sports, media, and business. For Rutledge, it came during a career stretch that will soon include one of the biggest assignments in American sports broadcasting when ESPN debuts its first Super Bowl coverage next February.
Article Summary: ESPN host Laura Rutledge shared photos from her trip to Cannes with Magic Johnson. The meeting brought together two of the biggest names in sports media and basketball. Rutledge is preparing for another milestone in her broadcasting career with ESPN. Johnson's presence reflects how his influence has continued to expand since retiring.

Article Title: "Show me a picture of them kissing": Two-time Super Bowl champ rejects Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel affair claims
Article Text:
Dianna Russini. (Getty Images)

Why is LeSean McCoy rejecting the allegations against Dianna Russini?

The Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy refuses to die down. New York Times published a detailed report this week revealing that Russini was earning $800,000 annually at The Athletic before resigning in April, days after Page Six broke the first pictures of her and the New England Patriots head coach holding hands at an adults-only resort in Arizona. The Athletic has separately confirmed its investigation into Russini's journalistic conduct remains active.On Wednesday's episode of the Speakeasy podcast, two-time Super Bowl champion LeSean McCoy dismissed the Dianna Russini affair allegations outright, pointing to what he called a complete absence of concrete proof."I read what she said when she was resigning, and I think her integrity and her respect for her and her family is bigger than some money," McCoy said. "That's why she left, because they were dragging her family in it, her kids in it, and her husband in it — all because she wanted to hang out with a friend of hers. Ya'll want to play, but there hasn't been no true facts, for real. Some pictures that look [a certain way], a video that stopped and froze. I don't do all that."McCoy wasn't done. He went after the media's appetite for scandal, arguing that slowed-down footage and frozen frames don't constitute evidence of anything."I live in the gray area, and I know what's really going on. The media loves negativity. The media wants to talk bad about you. The media want to talk bad about you. They don't want to say anything good about her [or] about him. So, they slow a video down. 'Oh, look, they're kissing!' Unless I see a real video of him kissing her, of him grabbing her, of him doing something to her, I don't believe that. I don't believe that. Show me a picture of them kissing; you don't see no lip-on-lip action. "Former NFL linebacker Emmanuel Acho pushed back hard. His argument wasn't about rumour or innuendo. It was about the gaps in Dianna Russini's own defence."Dianna Russini resigns saying like, 'This is all a lie. This is all fabrication. Mike and I are just friends. We were there with six individuals. All of this is a lie,'" Acho said. "And The Athletic was like, 'Well, Dianna, where were the six individuals? Just prove them. We can't find them. Our investigation doesn't turn up any six friends.'"Acho then listed what he described as a mounting trail of circumstantial evidence."Now, there are more pictures about you and Mike Vrabel," he added. "There's a playlist of you and Mike Vrabel. There's a picture of you seven months pregnant on a boat with Mike Vrabel. You named your son Mike. There's too much evidence and empirical data that's going against you. So, that to me is why, like, she tried to play in everybody's face. I feel bad because an $800,000 job is still an $800,000 job. And in theory, Mike Vrabel really faced no occupational consequences besides missing a day of work. "That last point lands with some weight. Vrabel skipped Day 3 of the 2026 NFL Draft while addressing the fallout with his family, and he later admitted his "previous actions don't meet the standard that I hold myself to." He has not confirmed an affair, but the cryptic phrasing has fuelled more speculation than silence would have. The boating trip photographs from 2021, when Russini was pregnant and Vrabel was still coaching the Tennessee Titans, have only deepened the questions.McCoy and Acho are looking at the same set of facts and landing in entirely different places. What's clear is that neither Russini nor Vrabel has fully closed the door on this story.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini resigned from her $800,000-a-year job at The Athletic in April. She was accused of having an affair with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. LeSean McCoy says there is no evidence of an affair between the two.
